name.value: Accessible University (AU) is a fictional university home page designed to demonstrate a variety of common web design problems
that result in visitors with disabilities being unable to access content or features. AU was originally developed by
, both projects based out of the University of Washington. Use the AU site to:
demonstrate common web accessibility principles at trainings, presentations, and workshops on accessible web design.

name.value: demonstrates common web accessibility issues using the fictitious AU home page. See how many web accessibility problems you can identify. NOTE: Since the purpose of this page is to demonstrate inaccessible web design, certain features are inherently inaccessible to some groups of users.

name.value: Additional Files for Demonstrating Document Accessibility
In addition to the AU web pages, we have created two sets of digital documents that can be used for demonstrating document
accessibility issues in presentations and workshops. Links are provided below for downloading the files. For details about each file,
Each of the files in this section contains the same content, a syllabus from a fictional
Microsoft Word and PDF formats, with varying levels of accessibility.
The files in this section are both PowerPoint files, with and without accessibility problems.

necessarily represent the policies of the U.S. federal government, and you should not assume their endorsement.
